questions.question: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: She (3rd person singular) के साथ verb में 's' लगता है: go→goes।

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'They ___ my friends.'
questions.explanation: They (plural) के साथ 'are' आता है।

questions.question: Which article is correct? 'She is ___ engineer.'
questions.explanation: 'Engineer' vowel sound (e) से शुरू होता है, इसलिए 'an' आता है।

questions.question: Which is correct negative form?
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) के साथ 'doesn't' + base verb आता है।

questions.question: Choose the correct question form:
questions.explanation: She के साथ 'Does' + subject + base verb।

questions.question: Which pronoun correctly replaces 'Rahul'? 'Rahul is my friend. ___ is very funny.'
questions.explanation: Rahul एक male name है, इसलिए 'He' pronoun use होगा।

questions.question: 'I never drink alcohol.' — Frequency adverb 'never' means:
questions.explanation: 'Never' = कभी नहीं = 0% frequency।

questions.question: Which possessive adjective is correct? '___ mother is a doctor. (She)'
questions.explanation: She → Her (possessive adjective)।

questions.question: Which is the correct object pronoun? 'Please help ___ (I)'
questions.explanation: Object position में I → me।

questions.question: 'She ___ to the gym every morning.' Choose the correct form of 'go'.
questions.explanation: She (3rd person singular) + go → goes।

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'the' correctly?
options: I ate the apple this morning.
The sun rises in the east.
questions.explanation: 'The sun' — there is only one sun, so we use 'the'. Sports names don't take articles. 'An apple' (first mention), 'a brother' (not specific).

questions.question: What is the WH question for: 'I live in Delhi.'
questions.explanation: Place के बारे में पूछने के लिए 'Where' use करते हैं।

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Frequency adverbs (always) 'be' verb (is) के बाद और main verb से पहले आते हैं।

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: 'Umbrella' vowel sound (u) से शुरू होता है → 'an umbrella'।

questions.question: Translate: 'मैं हर रोज़ सुबह दौड़ता हूँ।'
questions.explanation: I के साथ base verb 'run' (no s/es). 'Every morning' = time expression.

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या वह Hindi बोलती है?'
questions.explanation: She के साथ 'Does' + subject + base verb। Does के बाद base form 'speak' (speaks नहीं)।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह मेरा भाई है।'
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) + is. 'my brother' = possessive adjective + noun।

questions.question: Translate: 'वे English नहीं बोलते।'
questions.explanation: They (plural) के साथ 'don't' + base verb 'speak'।

questions.question: Translate: 'यह एक बड़ा घर है।'
questions.explanation: 'House' consonant sound से शुरू → 'a'. This → is (singular)।

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'My sister ___ in a hospital.' (work)
questions.explanation: My sister = 3rd person singular → works।

questions.question: Fill in the blank: '___ you understand this lesson?'
questions.explanation: You के साथ 'Do' use होता है question बनाने के लिए।

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'She ___ like spicy food.'
questions.explanation: She (3rd person singular) के साथ negative में 'doesn't' + base verb।

questions.question: Fill in: 'I see ___ every day.' (she)
questions.explanation: Object position में 'she' → 'her'।

questions.question: Fill in: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.explanation: 'Honest' की आवाज़ vowel (o) से शुरू होती है (h silent) → 'an'।

questions.question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: We are usually go by bus.
questions.explanation: We + base verb (go). Frequency adverb 'usually' main verb से पहले आता है।

questions.question: Choose the correct possessive adjective: 'The dog wagged ___ tail.'
questions.explanation: Animal या thing के लिए 'its' (possessive) use होता है।

questions.question: Which is the correct negative?
questions.explanation: 'Am' का negative 'am not' होता है। Be verb के साथ don't/doesn't नहीं आता।

questions.question: What does 'usually' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Usually' = ज़्यादातर समय, आमतौर पर। 80-90% of the time।

questions.question: 'She don't go to school.' — This sentence is correct.
questions.explanation: She (3rd person singular) के साथ 'doesn't' आता है, 'don't' नहीं। Correct: She doesn't go to school.

questions.question: Which is the correct question?
questions.explanation: Name पूछने के लिए 'What is your name?' standard form है।

questions.question: 'I ___ to music while I exercise.' (listen) — which is correct?
questions.explanation: I के साथ Simple Present में base verb 'listen' (no s)।

questions.question: Which sentence has the WRONG verb form?
options: She go to college by bus.
We work in the same office.
questions.explanation: 'She' के साथ 'go' नहीं, 'goes' आना चाहिए।

questions.question: Translate: 'तुम्हारा घर कहाँ है?'
questions.explanation: WH question: Where + is + your + noun? 'House' singular → is।

questions.question: '___ book is this? It is ___.' (yours/mine)
questions.explanation: Ownership पूछने के लिए 'Whose', answer में object के बाद 'mine' (possessive pronoun)।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'often'?
options: Often she goes to the gym.
She often goes to the gym.
She goes often to the gym.
She goes to often the gym.
questions.explanation: 'Often' main verb 'goes' से पहले आता है: She often goes.

questions.question: What is the subject in: 'My elder brother works in Hyderabad.'
questions.explanation: Subject = जो काम कर रहा है = 'My elder brother'।

questions.question: Which question word asks about time?
questions.explanation: 'When' = कब — time के बारे में पूछने के लिए।

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Own के साथ possessive adjective: my/your/his/her/our/their own।

questions.question: 'He always comes late to office.' — 'always' is correctly placed here.
questions.explanation: हाँ, 'always' यहाँ सही position पर है — main verb 'comes' से पहले।

questions.question: Which is correct? 'There ___ a lot of students in the class.'
questions.explanation: 'Students' plural noun है → 'There are'।

questions.question: Choose the correct option: 'He is ___ student in the class.'
questions.explanation: Superlative adjective (best) के साथ 'the' आता है।

questions.question: Translate: 'मुझे अंग्रेज़ी समझ नहीं आती।'
questions.explanation: I के साथ negative: I + don't + base verb।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses frequency adverb?
questions.explanation: Be verb (is) के बाद frequency adverb: She is never on time.

questions.question: '___ he know the answer?' — Choose correct auxiliary.
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) + Does + base verb।

questions.question: What does 'rarely' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Rarely' = बहुत कम बार, शायद ही कभी। Less frequent than 'sometimes'।

questions.question: Choose the correct object pronoun: 'She sent a message to ___.' (we)
questions.explanation: Preposition के बाद object pronoun: we → us।

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या वे रोज़ gym जाते हैं?'
options: Does they go to gym daily?
Do they goes to gym daily?
Do they go to the gym daily?
Are they go to gym daily?
questions.explanation: They के साथ 'Do'. 'Gym' के पहले 'the'. Base verb 'go' (not goes)।

questions.question: Which sentence has an article error?
options: I want to join the army.
questions.explanation: 'Honest' की sound vowel (o) से शुरू होती है → 'an honest man'।

questions.question: 'The teacher explained it to ___.' (we) — correct form:
questions.explanation: Verb + to + object pronoun: we → us।

questions.question: Which is grammatically correct?
questions.explanation: 'Have' एक stative verb है — इसे continuous form में use नहीं करते (am having गलत है possession के लिए)।

questions.question: Choose the correct question: 'You are from Kolkata.' → Question?
questions.explanation: WH question with be verb: WH word + be verb + subject? → Where are you from?

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरी माँ हमेशा सुबह जल्दी उठती हैं।'
options: My mother always wake up early in the morning.
My mother always wakes up early in the morning.
My mother wake up always early in the morning.
My mother always wakes up early in morning.
questions.explanation: Mother (3rd person singular) → wakes. Always = before main verb. 'The morning' = article ज़रूरी नहीं यहाँ।

questions.question: Which is correct: 'She is ___ most intelligent student.'
questions.explanation: Superlative के साथ हमेशा 'the' आता है: the most intelligent।

questions.question: '___ you like your job?' — Correct auxiliary:
questions.explanation: You के साथ 'Do' use होता है question बनाने के लिए।

questions.question: Identify the object in: 'My father reads the newspaper every morning.'
questions.explanation: Object = verb के बाद जो noun आता है = 'the newspaper'।

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: 'Agree' एक stative verb है। 'am agree' गलत है। Simple: I agree।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह कौन सी language सबसे ज़्यादा बोलती है?'
options: What language she speaks the most?
Which language does she speak the most?
Which language she speaks the most?
What language does she speak most?
questions.explanation: WH + does + subject + base verb? 'Which' specific options में से choice के लिए।

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence with 'sometimes':
questions.explanation: 'Sometimes' आमतौर पर main verb से पहले आता है। Option A और C दोनों valid हैं लेकिन A more natural/standard है।

questions.question: 'Does your father work in a bank?' — This question is correctly formed.
questions.explanation: Father (singular) → Does + subject + base verb work। सही है।

questions.question: Which pronoun correctly replaces 'Priya and Meena'?
questions.explanation: दो या अधिक लोगों के लिए 'They' use करते हैं।

questions.question: Which is correct? 'He ___ the office at 9 AM every day.'
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) + reach → reaches (ch ending → add es)।

questions.question: 'She always ___ the right thing.' (do) — Correct form:
questions.explanation: She (3rd person singular) + do → does।

questions.question: Translate: 'हम एक बड़े परिवार में रहते हैं।'
options: We live in a big family.
We lives in a big family.
We are live in big family.
We live in big a family.
questions.explanation: We + base verb (live). 'A big family' = article + adjective + noun। Article adjective से पहले आता है।

questions.question: Which article usage is wrong?
options: I need a hour to finish.
questions.explanation: 'Hour' की sound vowel (ow) से शुरू होती है (h silent) → 'an hour'।

questions.question: What is the correct negative: 'She is happy.'?
questions.explanation: Be verb (is) का negative 'is not' = 'isn't' होता है।

questions.question: Choose the right sentence:
questions.explanation: Negative sentence में double negative (don't + no) गलत होता है। 'Don't + any' correct है।

questions.question: Translate: 'तुम्हारा favorite singer कौन है?'
questions.explanation: Person के बारे में पूछने के लिए 'Who'. WH + is + your + noun?

questions.question: 'He ___ never ___ a class.' (miss)
questions.explanation: 'Never' के साथ simple present: He + never + base verb (miss, not misses because does is already there)। Wait — subject + never + verb+s is actually correct: 'He never misses' is also valid. But 'He does never miss' is wrong. Best: 'He never misses a class.'

questions.question: Which has the correct SVO order?
questions.explanation: SVO = Subject (I) + Verb (eat) + Object (rice) + Time (every day)। English में यही basic order है।

questions.question: Which sentence is correct? (Object pronoun test)
options: She gave the book to I.
She gave the book to me.
She gave the book to my.
She gave the book to mine.
questions.explanation: Preposition के बाद object pronoun: I → me।

questions.question: 'He ___ to office on Saturdays.' Which is correct?
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) → doesn't + base verb go।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे पास कोई problem नहीं है।'
options: I am not having any problems.
questions.explanation: Negative के साथ 'any' use होता है (not no, which creates double negative). I + don't + have + any।

questions.question: Correct the error: 'She often is late.'
questions.explanation: Frequency adverb 'often' be verb के बाद आता है: She is often late।

questions.question: 'Why ___ she always ___ angry?' (be)
questions.explanation: Why does + subject + base verb? → Why does she always be? — though more natural is 'Why is she always angry?' which uses be verb directly.

questions.question: Which is NOT a frequency adverb?
questions.explanation: 'Very' एक degree adverb है, frequency adverb नहीं। always, never, sometimes = frequency adverbs।

questions.question: Choose correct: 'Our team ___ very well together.'
questions.explanation: 'Our team' = singular unit → works।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह कभी झूठ नहीं बोलता।'
options: He doesn't never tell a lie.
questions.explanation: 'Never' के साथ doesn't नहीं चाहिए (double negative)। He + never + tells (3rd person verb)। 'Tell a lie' = lie बोलना।

questions.question: Which question is correctly formed?
options: How much does a mango cost?
How much does a mango costs?
How much is a mango cost?
questions.explanation: How much + does + subject + base verb? → How much does a mango cost?

questions.question: 'I am knowing the answer.' is a correct English sentence.
questions.explanation: 'Know' एक stative verb है। Stative verbs continuous form में नहीं आते। Correct: 'I know the answer.'

questions.question: Choose the most natural sentence:
questions.explanation: Time expression 'daily' sentence के end में सबसे natural लगती है: I go to gym daily.

questions.question: Translate: 'उसकी बहन doctor नहीं है।'
options: His sister don't be a doctor.
questions.explanation: Sister = singular noun, is → isn't (negative of is). 'A doctor' = article + noun।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'their'?
questions.explanation: 'Their' possessive adjective है: their + noun। 'Their is' wrong (there is — different word)।

questions.question: Which correctly shows SVO structure?
questions.explanation: SVO: She (S) + drinks (V) + tea (O) + every morning (time) — correct English order।

questions.question: '___ your parents always support you?'
questions.explanation: 'Parents' plural noun → 'Do' use होगा।

questions.question: Which is the correct negative question?
questions.explanation: She (singular) → Doesn't + subject + base verb (work, not works)।

questions.question: Translate: 'तुम कितनी बार gym जाते हो?'
options: How much do you go to gym?
How many times do you go to the gym?
How often do you go to the gym?
questions.explanation: Frequency पूछने के लिए 'How often' use करते हैं।

questions.question: Which sentence has a possessive error?
options: Our house is near the park.
questions.explanation: 'Their is beautiful' — 'their' possessive adjective है, इसके बाद noun ज़रूरी है। 'There is' (अलग शब्द) या 'Their house is beautiful' होना चाहिए।

questions.question: He ___ to the market. She ___ to the gym. (go — correct forms)
questions.explanation: He → goes, She → goes। दोनों 3rd person singular हैं।

questions.question: 'I am never late.' is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: हाँ, 'never' be verb (am) के बाद आता है: I am never late। यह correct है।

questions.question: Which is the correct form for a negative WH question?
options: Why you don't come to class?
Why don't you come to class?
Why you come not to class?
Why not you come to class?
questions.explanation: Negative WH question: WH + don't/doesn't + subject + base verb?

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या उसे English आती है?'
questions.explanation: He (singular) → Does + subject + base verb know।

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: 'Rarely' be verb के बाद आता है: She is rarely absent।

questions.question: 'I invited ___ to the party.' (they)
questions.explanation: Object position में they → them।

questions.question: Which has correct article usage?
questions.explanation: 'MBA' vowel sound (em) से शुरू होता है → 'an MBA'।

questions.question: Choose the correct form: 'How ___ he come to office?'
questions.explanation: He (3rd person singular) → Does + he + base verb?

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरी बहन हमेशा office में सबसे पहले आती है।'
options: My sister always comes first to the office.
My sister always come first to office.
My sister always comes first to office.
My sister is always comes first in office.
questions.explanation: Sister (singular) → comes. Always main verb से पहले। 'The office' specific place है।

questions.question: Which is wrong?
options: She usually comes to work by metro.
questions.explanation: 'We' plural → 'go' (not 'goes')। We sometimes go to movies।

questions.question: Choose the correct complex sentence:
options: Although she works hard but she doesn't succeed.
Although she works hard, she doesn't succeed.
Although she works hard, she doesn't succeeds.
Although she work hard, she doesn't succeed.
questions.explanation: 'Although...,' — no 'but' after although. She (singular) → works. Doesn't + base verb succeed।

questions.question: Translate: 'हमारे office में हर employee को English बोलनी पड़ती है।'
options: In our office every employee have to speak English.
In our office, every employee has to speak English.
In our office, every employee must speaks English.
Our office every employee speaks English.
questions.explanation: 'Every employee' = singular → has to. 'Has to' = obligation। Must + base verb (not speaks)।

questions.question: What is the SVO structure of: 'The manager gives the team new assignments every Monday.'?
options: S=The manager, V=gives, O=the team new assignments
S=The manager, V=gives, O=new assignments every Monday
questions.explanation: Subject = The manager, Verb = gives, Direct Object = new assignments. 'The team' is indirect object, 'every Monday' is time adverb.

questions.question: Translate: 'वह rarely meeting में late आती है लेकिन आज वह late है।'
options: She rarely comes late to meeting but today she is late.
She rarely come late to meeting but today she is late.
She is rarely late to meeting but today she comes late.
She rarely comes late to meetings but today she is late.
questions.explanation: She → comes (singular). 'Meetings' plural better in general context. 'Is late' for current state.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly combines subject + frequency adverb + be verb + adjective?
questions.explanation: Subject + be verb + frequency adverb + adjective: She is always confident।

questions.question: 'Who ___ responsible for this project?' — correct auxiliary:
questions.explanation: 'Who' singular subject के रूप में treat होता है when asking about identity → is।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे manager मुझ पर trust करते हैं।'
options: My manager is trust on me.
questions.explanation: 'Trust' + object (no preposition 'on' in English). Manager (singular) → trusts।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses possessive adjective in a professional context?
options: I submitted my report on time.
I submitted her report on time.
I submitted the my report on time.
I submitted report my on time.
questions.explanation: Possessive adjective (my) noun से पहले आता है, बिना article के: my report।

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या हर employee अपनी attendance खुद mark करता है?'
options: Does every employee marks their own attendance?
Does every employee mark their own attendance?
Do every employee mark their own attendance?
Does every employee marking their attendance?
questions.explanation: 'Every employee' singular → Does. Does के बाद base verb 'mark' (not marks)। 'Their own' = gender-neutral possessive।

questions.question: Which sentence has BOTH article and verb form errors?
options: He goes to the office daily.
questions.explanation: 'She have' wrong (should be 'She has'). 'A cat' is correct. So only verb error here.

questions.question: Choose the sentence with CORRECT use of all grammar rules:
options: She usually doesn't speaks in meetings.
She usually doesn't speak in meetings.
She usually don't speak in meetings.
She usually not speak in meetings.
questions.explanation: She → doesn't (not don't). Doesn't + base verb speak (not speaks). Usually before main verb phrase.

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे सभी colleagues बहुत supportive हैं।'
options: My all colleagues are very supportive.
All my colleagues are very supportive.
My colleagues all are very supportive.
All of my colleague are very supportive.
questions.explanation: 'All my colleagues' — 'all' quantifier + possessive + noun. 'All my' is more natural than 'my all'.

questions.question: In the sentence 'She rarely visits her parents on weekdays', what is the frequency of her visits?
questions.explanation: 'Rarely' = बहुत कम बार, almost never = शायद ही कभी।

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या हमें अभी यह काम करना है?'
options: Do we have to do this work now?
Do we must do this work now?
Are we have to do this work now?
Must we do this work now?
questions.explanation: 'Have to' obligation express करता है। Do + we + have to + base verb। Both A and D are correct but 'Do we have to' is more common in conversation.

questions.question: Choose the sentence with correct SVO + frequency adverb placement:
options: I always my homework do after dinner.
I do always my homework after dinner.
I always do my homework after dinner.
After dinner always I do my homework.
questions.explanation: Subject + always + main verb + object + time: I always do my homework after dinner।

questions.question: 'Every morning, the team ___ for the daily standup.' (assemble)
questions.explanation: 'The team' = singular unit → assembles। Even though team has many members, 'the team' is one entity.

questions.question: Which question is INCORRECTLY formed?
options: What does he do for a living?
How does she usually comes to work?
questions.explanation: 'How does she usually comes' — after does, base verb आती है: 'How does she usually come to work?'

questions.question: Translate: 'उसकी English उसके काम में बड़ी मदद करती है।'
options: His English helps him a lot in his work.
His English help him a lot in his work.
His English is help him in work.
His English helping him in his work.
questions.explanation: 'His English' singular subject → helps. 'Him' object pronoun. 'In his work' prepositional phrase.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly expresses habit using Simple Present?
options: She is going to the gym every day.
She goes to the gym every day.
She was going to the gym every day.
She has gone to the gym every day.
questions.explanation: Habit express करने के लिए Simple Present tense use करते हैं। 'She goes' = regular habit।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे boss कभी भी किसी पर चिल्लाते नहीं हैं।'
options: My boss never shout on anyone.
My boss never shouts at anyone.
My boss doesn't never shout at anyone.
My boss is never shouting at anyone.
questions.explanation: Boss (singular) → shouts. Never = frequency adverb before main verb. Shout 'at' (not 'on') someone. No double negative.

questions.question: Choose the grammatically perfect professional sentence:
options: Our company provide good salary and benefits to it's employees.
Our company provides good salary and benefits to its employees.
Our company provides good salary and benefits to their employees.
Our companies provide good salary and benefits to its employees.
questions.explanation: Company (singular) → provides. 'its' (possessive) not 'it's' (it is). 'Its employees' refers back to the company.

questions.question: 'Does every student understand the lesson?' — The verb 'understand' is correctly used as base form here.
questions.explanation: हाँ, 'Does' के बाद base verb आती है: Does + subject + base verb. 'Understand' base form है — 'understands' नहीं।

questions.question: Identify the correct sentence with object pronoun:
options: Between you and I, this is wrong.
Between you and me, this is wrong.
Between you and mine, this is wrong.
Between you and myself, this is wrong.
questions.explanation: Preposition 'between' के बाद object pronoun: I → me। 'Between you and me' correct है।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह जो भी करती है, बहुत अच्छे से करती है।'
options: Whatever she does, she does it very well.
Whatever she do, she does very well.
Whatever she does, she do it very well.
questions.explanation: She → does (singular). 'She does it very well' — object pronoun 'it' जो 'whatever she does' को refer करता है।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'its' vs 'it's'?
options: The book has its own charm.
questions.explanation: 'Its' = possessive (का/की/के), 'It's' = it is। 'The book has its own charm' — its = belonging to the book।

questions.question: Which has BOTH pronoun and verb errors?
options: She and him goes to gym.
He and she go to the gym.
Him and she go to the gym.
Him and her goes to the gym.
questions.explanation: 'Him and her' — subject position में object pronouns। Plus 'goes' with plural subject. Correct: He and she go.

questions.question: Translate: 'मैं उन्हें personally जानता हूँ।'
questions.explanation: Object position में they → them. 'Know' stative verb — no continuous form. I + know (base).

questions.question: Choose the sentence that follows NATURAL English word order:
options: She every morning exercises in the park.
Every morning exercises she in the park.
She exercises in the park every morning.
In the park she every morning exercises.
questions.explanation: Natural English: Subject + Verb + Place + Time. She exercises in the park every morning.

questions.question: 'The HR team ___ all the interviews this week.' (conduct)
questions.explanation: 'The HR team' = singular entity → conducts।

questions.question: Translate: 'जो लोग कड़ी मेहनत करते हैं वे हमेशा succeed करते हैं।'
options: People who work hard always succeed.
Peoples who works hard always succeeds.
People who work hard always succeeds.
The people who work hard always succeed.
questions.explanation: 'People' plural → work, succeed. 'Who work hard' relative clause. 'Always' before main verb succeed.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a dangling modifier error?
options: Working hard, she earns a lot.
She works hard and earns a lot.
Because she works hard, she succeeds.
questions.explanation: 'Working hard, it pays off' — 'it' cannot work hard. The subject performing the action of 'working hard' should be the sentence subject.

questions.question: Choose the grammatically correct sentence for a job interview:
options: I always gives my best in everything I do.
I always give my best in everything I do.
I always given my best in everything I do.
I always giving my best in everything I do.
questions.explanation: I + always + base verb (give). 'In everything I do' = relative clause.

questions.question: Translate: 'हमारी company rarely अपने targets miss करती है।'
options: Our company rarely misses its targets.
Our company rarely miss its targets.
Our company is rarely missing its targets.
Our company rarely misses their targets.
questions.explanation: Company (singular) → misses. 'Rarely' before main verb. 'Its targets' = company's own targets (its, not their).

questions.question: Which correctly uses DO as a main verb (not auxiliary)?
options: I do my best every day.
questions.explanation: 'I do my best' — यहाँ 'do' main verb है (perform/करना)। बाकी sentences में do/does auxiliary हैं।

questions.question: What error does this sentence have? 'She don't usually comes to work on Mondays.'
questions.explanation: She → doesn't (not don't). After doesn't, base verb: come (not comes).

questions.question: Translate: 'आज का meeting का agenda क्या है?'
options: What is the agenda for today's meeting?
questions.explanation: 'What is the agenda for today's meeting?' — 'for today's meeting' prepositional phrase। 'The agenda' specific item।

questions.question: Choose the error-free professional sentence:
options: My team and me works on this project.
My team and I work on this project.
Me and my team works on this project.
I and my team work on this project.
questions.explanation: Subject position में 'I' (not 'me'). Plural subject 'my team and I' → work. 'I' should come last in polite English.

questions.question: Which correctly uses WH word + does?
questions.explanation: WH + does + subject + frequency adverb + base verb. 'Work' base form after does.

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या वह manager है या employee?'
options: Is she a manager or an employee?
Is she a manager or a employee?
Does she a manager or an employee?
She is a manager or an employee?
questions.explanation: Be verb question: Is + subject + noun? 'A manager' (m = consonant), 'an employee' (e = vowel sound).

questions.question: Which sentence has the MOST errors?
questions.explanation: 'I are a teacher' — I + am (not are). Multiple grammar rules violated. 'She doesn't speaks' has 1 error, 'He don't know' has 1 error, 'They is' has 1 error.

questions.question: Translate: 'मैं कभी भी किसी के साथ dishonest नहीं रहा।'
options: I am never dishonest with anyone.
I never am dishonest with anyone.
I always am honest with everyone.
I never been dishonest with anyone.
questions.explanation: I am + never (after be verb) + adjective. 'With anyone' negative context में 'anyone' use होता है।

questions.question: Choose the sentence with correct article + superlative:
options: She is the best employee here.
She is a best employee here.
She is an best employee here.
questions.explanation: Superlative (best) के साथ हमेशा 'the' आता है: the best।

questions.question: Which is NOT correct in formal English?
options: I would like to introduce myself.
Me want to ask a question.
questions.explanation: Subject position में object pronoun 'me' use नहीं होता। Correct: 'I want to ask a question.'

questions.question: Translate: 'वह हमारी team का सबसे experienced member है।'
options: He is the most experienced member of our team.
He is most experienced member of our team.
He is the more experienced member of our team.
He is an experienced member of our team.
questions.explanation: 'The most experienced' = superlative + article 'the'. 'Of our team' = possessive phrase.

questions.question: What is the correct question for: 'She works 10 hours a day.'?
questions.explanation: Duration/time पूछने के लिए 'How long'. Does + she + base verb work?

questions.question: Choose the error-free sentence:
options: Neither she nor he are responsible.
Either she or he is responsible.
Both she or he are responsible.
Neither she or he is responsible.
questions.explanation: 'Either...or': verb agrees with the nearest subject (he → is). 'Neither...nor', 'Either...or', 'Both...and' — correct correlatives।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे पास इस project के लिए पर्याप्त समय नहीं है।'
options: I don't have enough time for this project.
I don't have sufficient time to this project.
I am not having enough time for this project.
I doesn't have enough time for this project.
questions.explanation: I + don't + have (stative verb). 'Enough time' = sufficient time. 'For this project' preposition.

questions.question: Which sentence uses pronouns most correctly throughout?
options: My brother and me go to the same school.
My brother and I go to the same school.
Me and my brother goes to the same school.
I and my brother go to the same school.
questions.explanation: Subject position में 'I'. Plural → go. Polite order: others first, then 'I'. 'My brother and I' is correct.

questions.question: Translate: 'हमारे director rarely गलत decisions लेते हैं।'
options: Our director rarely takes wrong decisions.
Our director rarely take wrong decisions.
Our director is rarely taking wrong decisions.
Our director never takes right decisions.
questions.explanation: Director (singular) → takes. 'Rarely' frequency adverb before main verb. 'Wrong decisions' = incorrect choices.

questions.question: Choose the sentence where 'do' is used as both auxiliary AND main verb:
options: Do you do your work well?
questions.explanation: 'Do you do your work well?' — first 'do' = auxiliary (question form), second 'do' = main verb (perform/करना)।

questions.question: Which sentence correctly handles pronoun reference?
options: When a student fails, they should try again.
When a student fails, he should try again.
When a student fails, it should try again.
When a student fails, she should try again.
questions.explanation: Modern English में gender-neutral singular pronoun 'they' use होता है जब gender unknown हो। 'They' is now accepted as singular.

questions.question: Translate: 'जब मैं English में बोलता हूँ, लोग मुझे seriously लेते हैं।'
options: When I speak in English, people take me seriously.
When I speak English, peoples take me seriously.
When I speak in English, people takes me seriously.
When I speaks English, people take me seriously.
questions.explanation: I + speak (base). People (plural) → take. 'Me' object pronoun. 'Seriously' adverb.

questions.question: Which correctly transforms 'She works every day.' into a question?
questions.explanation: She → Does. Does के बाद base verb: work (not works).

questions.question: What type of word is 'usually' in: 'I usually wake up at 7.'?
questions.explanation: 'Usually' frequency adverb है जो बताता है कितनी बार कोई action होता है।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह IT company में software developer है।'
options: She is a software developer in an IT company.
She is software developer in IT company.
She is an software developer in a IT company.
She works as software developer in IT company.
questions.explanation: 'A software developer' (s = consonant). 'An IT company' (I = vowel sound).

questions.question: Which word is an antonym of 'always'?
questions.explanation: 'Always' = हमेशा (100%), 'Never' = कभी नहीं (0%) — ये opposite हैं।

questions.question: 'The students ___ their teacher very much.' (respect)
questions.explanation: 'The students' plural → respect (base form, no s)।

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या यह सच है?'
questions.explanation: Be verb question: Is + this + adjective? 'True' adjective है, 'truth' noun।

questions.question: Choose correct: 'There ___ no easy way to success.'
questions.explanation: 'No easy way' singular → There is।

questions.question: Which correctly uses 'how' as a WH question word?
questions.explanation: 'How are you feeling?' — How + be verb + subject + participle। 'How is your name' wrong (What is your name)।

questions.question: In 'She doesn't know the answer', the main verb is 'know' in base form.
questions.explanation: हाँ, 'doesn't' auxiliary है और 'know' main verb base form में है।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे पिताजी daily newspaper पढ़ते हैं।'
options: My father reads the newspaper daily.
My father read the newspaper daily.
My father is reads newspaper daily.
My father does reads newspaper daily.
questions.explanation: Father (singular) → reads. 'The newspaper' specific item। 'Daily' time adverb।

questions.question: Which verb correctly completes: 'Our teacher always ___ difficult concepts simple.'?
questions.explanation: 'Our teacher' singular → makes।

questions.question: Choose the correct negative: 'We ___ English at home.'
questions.explanation: We (plural) → don't + base verb speak।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह घर पर नहीं है।'
questions.explanation: She + is not + at home। 'At home' = location preposition + noun।

questions.question: 'My aunt ___ in the USA.' (live)
questions.explanation: My aunt (3rd person singular) → lives।

questions.question: Which is the WH question word for 'reason/कारण'?
questions.explanation: 'Why' = क्यों — reason/cause पूछने के लिए।

questions.question: Translate: 'वे हर रविवार temple जाते हैं।'
options: They go to the temple every Sunday.
They goes to temple every Sunday.
They are go to temple every Sunday.
questions.explanation: They (plural) → go. 'The temple' specific place। 'Every Sunday' time expression।

questions.question: What does 'sometimes' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Sometimes' = कभी-कभी। It means occasionally, not always and not never।

questions.question: 'Do ___ parents know about this?' (she)
questions.explanation: She → her parents। Possessive adjective।

questions.question: Translate: 'मैं एक honest इंसान हूँ।'
questions.explanation: 'Honest' की sound vowel से शुरू (h silent) → 'an honest person'।

questions.question: Choose the correct form: 'What ___ your father do?'
questions.explanation: Your father (3rd person singular) → does।

questions.question: Which is NOT a subject pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'Him' object pronoun है (I → me, He → him)। Subject pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they।

questions.question: Translate: 'वे दोनों बहुत talented हैं।'
options: Both of them are very talented.
questions.explanation: 'Both of them' natural English expression। 'They both' भी possible है। 'Them both' (object pronoun as subject) incorrect।

questions.question: 'She ___ to music every morning while exercising.' (listen)
questions.explanation: She (singular) + listen → listens।

questions.question: Which is correct?
options: He is more smarter than me.
He is most smart than me.
questions.explanation: 'Smarter' already comparative form है। 'More smarter' double comparative है — गलत।

questions.question: Translate: 'मुझे नहीं पता वह कहाँ जाता है।'
options: I don't know where he goes.
I don't know where does he go.
I not know where he goes.
I don't know where he go.
questions.explanation: Indirect question में WH + subject + verb (no inversion): where he goes। He (singular) → goes।

questions.question: Choose the correct preposition: 'She is interested ___ learning English.'
questions.explanation: 'Interested in' + noun/gerund। 'Interested in learning' = सीखने में रुचि।

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या तुम्हें coffee पसंद है?'
questions.explanation: You → Do + you + base verb like?

questions.question: Which correctly uses 'Why doesn't'?
questions.explanation: doesn't के बाद base verb come (not comes/coming/came)।

questions.question: 'He never ___ a promise.' (break)
questions.explanation: He (singular) + never + breaks। 'Never' के साथ simple present।

questions.question: Translate: 'मेरे सभी दोस्त cricket खेलते हैं।'
options: All of my friends play cricket.
All of my friends plays cricket.
questions.explanation: 'All of my friends' plural → play. Natural order: All of my friends।

questions.question: Select the correct form for a formal introduction:
options: Me am Suresh Kumar from accounts department.
I am Suresh Kumar from the accounts department.
I is Suresh Kumar of accounts department.
Am I Suresh Kumar from accounts department.
questions.explanation: I + am + name + from + the + department। Formal introduction में 'the' + department name।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह कभी-कभी सुबह जल्दी office आता है।'
options: He sometimes comes to office early in the morning.
He comes sometimes early in morning.
He is sometimes coming early to office morning.
Sometimes he come to the office early.
questions.explanation: He → comes (singular). Sometimes before main verb. 'Early in the morning' time phrase.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly shows: subject + do/does + NOT for emphasis?
questions.explanation: I + do + not + base verb. 'I do not agree' — formal negative। Others have verb form errors.

questions.question: 'My company ___ its employees very well.' (treat)
questions.explanation: 'My company' singular → treats।

questions.question: Translate: 'वह generally काम से late घर आता है।'
options: He generally comes home late from work.
He comes home generally late from work.
He generally come home late from work.
Generally he comes home from late work.
questions.explanation: He → comes. 'Generally' (similar to usually) = main verb से पहले। 'Late from work' = time/manner।

questions.question: Which correctly asks about someone's profession?
options: What does he do for a living?
How does he do for a living?
questions.explanation: 'What does he do for a living?' — standard expression for asking about profession।

questions.question: Translate: 'मैं usually काम खुद करता हूँ।'
options: I usually do the work myself.
I do usually the work myself.
Usually I does the work myself.
questions.explanation: I + usually (before verb) + do (base) + work + myself। 'The work' specific work।

questions.question: What error is in: 'Our company provide excellent services to their clients.'?
questions.explanation: 'Our company' singular → provides. Company की clients → its clients (not their).

questions.question: Translate: 'क्या IT sector में jobs आसानी से मिलती हैं?'
options: Does jobs in IT sector come easily?
Do jobs come easily in the IT sector?
Do IT sector jobs comes easily?
Are jobs in IT sector easily?
questions.explanation: Jobs (plural) → Do. 'Come' base verb. 'The IT sector' specific industry।

questions.question: Complete with correct object pronoun: 'My boss always praises ___ in front of the team.' (I)
questions.explanation: Verb के बाद object position में I → me।

questions.question: 'He ___ always ___ the right thing.' (do)
questions.explanation: He (singular) + does (auxiliary for emphasis) + always + base verb do (main verb).

questions.question: Translate: 'आज हमारी पूरी team एक साथ काम कर रही है।'
options: Today, our entire team is working together.
Today our entire team works together.
Today, all our team is working together.
Today our whole team are working together.
questions.explanation: 'Team' singular → is working. 'Entire team' = पूरी team। 'Is working' present continuous for current action.

questions.question: Select the most formal/professional sounding sentence:
options: I don't got time for this.
I haven't got time for this.
I don't have time for this.
I ain't got time for this.
questions.explanation: 'I don't have time' = standard formal negative। 'Haven't got' is British English. 'Don't got' and 'ain't' are informal/incorrect.

questions.question: Translate: 'वह हमेशा अपना काम deadline से पहले finish करती है।'
options: She always finishes her work before the deadline.
She always finish her work before deadline.
She is always finishing her work before the deadline.
She always finishes work her before the deadline.
questions.explanation: She → finishes. Always before main verb. 'Her work' possessive. 'Before the deadline' prepositional phrase.

questions.question: Which correctly forms a negative question with 'Why'?
questions.explanation: Why + doesn't + subject + base verb? 'Why doesn't she understand?' correct form।

questions.question: Translate: 'हमारे office में हर कोई एक-दूसरे की help करता है।'
options: In our office, everyone helps each other.
In our office, everyone help each other.
In our office, everyone is helping each other.
In our office, every one helps each others.
questions.explanation: 'Everyone' singular → helps. 'Each other' (not 'each others') reciprocal pronoun। Simple Present for general truth.

questions.question: Choose the sentence where BOTH subject + verb agreement AND article usage are BOTH correct:
options: The manager give an feedback daily.
The manager gives a feedback daily.
questions.explanation: 'Manager' singular → gives. 'Feedback' uncountable noun — no article। 'The manager' specific person.

questions.question: Translate: 'जो लोग regularly practice करते हैं वे जल्दी improve करते हैं।'
options: People who practice regularly improve quickly.
People who regularly practices improve quickly.
Peoples who practice regularly improves quickly.
People who practices regularly improve quickly.
questions.explanation: People (plural) → practice, improve. 'Who practice regularly' = relative clause। 'Quickly' manner adverb।

questions.question: Which is the most natural and correct sentence for an interview?
options: I am very hard working and I always gives 100 percent.
I am a hardworking person and I always give my 100 percent.
I hardworking person and always giving 100 percent.
I am hardworking person and I gives 100 percent always.
questions.explanation: I + am + a + adjective+noun. I (subject) + always + give (base verb) + my 100 percent। 'Hardworking' compound adjective।

questions.question: Translate: 'मुझे यकीन है कि मैं यह कर सकता हूँ।'
options: I am sure that I can do this.
I am surely that I can do this.
I am sure to I can do this.
I sure that I can do this.
questions.explanation: 'I am sure that...' — 'sure' adjective after be verb. 'That' conjunction introduces the subordinate clause. 'Can do' = ability।

questions.explanation: Simple Present me subject "I" ke according verb form choose karo — he/she + verb+s, baaki subjects base verb.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ apple."
questions.explanation: "apple" vowel sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "an" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "मैं"?
questions.explanation: "I" subject pronoun hai, jabki "me" object aur "my" possessive hai।

questions.question: True or False: "I do not go every day." is a grammatically correct Simple Present Negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "I" ke saath "do not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.explanation: Do/Does subject se pehle aata hai, verb hamesha base form me rehti hai.

questions.question: Choose the correct professional/office sentence:
options: Please to send the email today.
questions.explanation: Imperative office request: Please + base verb + object.

questions.explanation: Simple Present me subject "you" ke according verb form choose karo — he/she + verb+s, baaki subjects base verb.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ banana."
questions.explanation: "banana" consonant sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "a" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "तुम"?
questions.explanation: "you" subject pronoun hai, jabki "you" object aur "your" possessive hai।

questions.question: True or False: "You do not go every day." is a grammatically correct Simple Present Negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "you" ke saath "do not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.explanation: Simple Present me subject "you" ke according verb form choose karo — he/she + verb+s, baaki subjects base verb.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ umbrella."
questions.explanation: "umbrella" vowel sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "an" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "वह (पुरुष)"?
questions.explanation: "he" subject pronoun hai, jabki "him" object aur "his" possessive hai।

questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "you" ke saath "do not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Choose the grammatically correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Simple Present me subject "he" ke according verb form choose karo — he/she + verb+s, baaki subjects base verb.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ book."
questions.explanation: "book" consonant sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "a" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "वह (स्त्री)"?
questions.explanation: "she" subject pronoun hai, jabki "her" object aur "her" possessive hai।

questions.question: True or False: "He does not go every day." is a grammatically correct Simple Present Negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "he" ke saath "does not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ egg."
questions.explanation: "egg" vowel sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "an" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "हम"?
questions.explanation: "we" subject pronoun hai, jabki "us" object aur "our" possessive hai।

questions.question: True or False: "She does not go every day." is a grammatically correct Simple Present Negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "she" ke saath "does not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Choose the correct question form:
questions.explanation: Do/Does subject se pehle aata hai, verb hamesha base form me rehti hai.

questions.question: Choose the correct professional/office sentence:
options: Please to send the deadline today.
questions.explanation: Imperative office request: Please + base verb + object.

questions.question: Choose the grammatically correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Simple Present me subject "we" ke according verb form choose karo — he/she + verb+s, baaki subjects base verb.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "I have ___ chair."
questions.explanation: "chair" consonant sound se shuru hota hai, इसलिए "a" सही है।

questions.question: Which word is the SUBJECT pronoun for "वे"?
questions.explanation: "they" subject pronoun hai, jabki "them" object aur "their" possessive hai।

questions.question: True or False: "We do not go every day." is a grammatically correct Simple Present Negative sentence.
questions.explanation: Sahi hai: "we" ke saath "do not" + base verb sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Choose the correct question form:
questions.explanation: Do/Does subject se pehle aata hai, verb hamesha base form me rehti hai.

questions.question: Choose the correct professional/office sentence:
options: Please send the team meeting today.
Please to send the team meeting today.
Please sending the team meeting today.
Please sends the team meeting today.
